Apple___Apple_scab.treatment: Remove infected leaves, improve air circulation, and apply appropriate fungicide such as Captan or Mancozeb.

Apple___Black_rot.treatment: Remove infected fruits and branches, prune affected areas, and apply recommended fungicide.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.treatment: Remove infected leaves and apply suitable fungicide during early infection stages.

Apple___healthy.treatment: No treatment required. Continue regular monitoring and maintain proper plant nutrition.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.treatment: Remove infected plant debris, maintain crop spacing, and apply suitable fungicide if required.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.treatment: Apply recommended fungicide, use resistant varieties, and maintain proper field management.

Corn_(maize)___healthy.treatment: No treatment required. Maintain proper irrigation and nutrient management.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.treatment: Remove infected leaves, use resistant varieties, and apply suitable fungicide.

Pepper__bell___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericide, remove infected leaves, and avoid overhead irrigation.

Pepper__bell___healthy.treatment: No treatment required. Continue regular monitoring.

Potato___Early_blight.treatment: Apply Mancozeb or Chlorothalonil fungicide and remove infected foliage.

Potato___Late_blight.treatment: Apply appropriate fungicide immediately, remove infected plants, and avoid excess moisture.

Potato___healthy.treatment: No treatment required. Maintain proper soil nutrients and irrigation.

Tomato_Bacterial_spot.treatment: Use copper-based bactericide, remove infected leaves, and avoid wet foliage.

Tomato_Early_blight.treatment: Apply Mancozeb or Chlorothalonil fungicide and remove affected leaves.

Tomato_Late_blight.treatment: Apply Metalaxyl-based fungicide, isolate infected plants, and improve ventilation.

Tomato_Leaf_Mold.treatment: Improve greenhouse ventilation, reduce humidity, and apply suitable fungicide.

Tomato_Septoria_leaf_spot.treatment: Remove infected leaves, avoid overhead watering, and apply Chlorothalonil fungicide.

Tomato_Spider_mites_Two_spotted_spider_m.treatment: Apply neem oil or recommended miticide and monitor pest population regularly.
